node.content:
What do you get when a client has such good professional service that they seem to be able to pull off almost anything? You create a television campaign that says their guys (and women) are magicians.
 
This is exactly what The PM Group did when we created one of two :30 television commercials for our newest client A&E Air Conditioning and Heating L.L.C.
 
The commercials were released along with the new A&E Air Conditioning and Heating website. The PM Group will continue doing broadcast and web advertising for A&E.
 
Watch the “Magician” commercial below.

node.content:
April 9th was national Reveal Day for ChildSafe’s community-wide Cardboard Kids campaign. This was the second year that Cardboard Kids where adopted and decorated to “put a face” on child abuse.
 
 
The staff at The PM Group, The Wood Agency and QMP created and adopted their own Kids. But we were not alone. Over 20,000 Cardboard kids were placed in and around San Antonio – this was up from 5,600 in 2014. According to ChildSafe, the social media campaign netted 2 million social media impressions. Valero Energy Corp was a major sponsor that had a huge impact on promoting the Cardboard Kids endeavor.
 
Needless to say, both ChildSafe and The PM Group were thrilled by the results and look forward to new ways to bring focus to help promote Child Safety in our community.

node.content: Today is Reveal Day for ChildSafe’s community-wide Cardboard Kids campaign. As part of April, National Child Abuse Prevention Month, ChildSafe created Cardboard Kids to achieve a new level of awareness of child abuse by “putting a face” on it and engaging the community. The staff at The PM Group and The Wood Agency have done their part by adopting and creating their own Kids. It was fun and more importantly it brings the disturbing issue of child abuse to the public in hopes that we can reduce the rising number of cases locally. If you see a Cardboard Kid around San Antonio on Thursday, April 9, please read the information on the back, then take a picture of the Cardboard Kid and share him or her on your favorite social network with the hashtag #CardboardKidsSA.

node.content:
ChildSafe kicks-off National Child Abuse Prevention Month with dignitaries, partners and declares community-wide Cardboard Kids ‘Reveal’ Day
 
ChildSafe, Bexar County’s only Children’s Advocacy Center, will announce April as Child Abuse Prevention Month with dignitaries and local partners on Thursday, April 9, 1:30 pm at Brackenridge Park, Joske Pavilion, in addition to declaring that day as ‘Reveal Day’ for its community-wide Cardboard Kids campaign.
 
Those who work diligently on behalf of San Antonio’s families and children are part of the press conference, including: Kim Abernethy, ChildSafe CEO; Judge John Specia, Commissioner of Texas Department of Family and Protective Services; Susan Pamerleau, Bexar County Sheriff; Nico LaHood, Bexar County District Attorney; Anthony Treviño, San Antonio Police Chief, and Laura Vaccaro, Vice President, Community Relations of Valero Energy Foundation, this year’s sponsor of the Cardboard Kids campaign.
 
“Day in and day out, these individuals have the best interest of our children, families and community at heart, and we are standing together to show solidarity against child predators,” said Kim Abernethy, ChildSafe CEO.
 
As part of April, National Child Abuse Prevention Month, ChildSafe created Cardboard Kids to achieve a new level of awareness of child abuse by “putting a face” on it and engaging the community. Now in its second year, over 20,000 Cardboard Kids were distributed, representing the thousands of abused and neglected children in our community.
 
“Unfortunately, child abuse occurs across every demographic; it knows no boundaries, it happens everywhere,” said Kim Abernethy, ChildSafe CEO. “So ChildSafe created Cardboard Kids to illustrate the pervasiveness of the issue and the fact that every story is different and every child unique,” she said.
 
Citizens are asked to join in this effort by taking a photo of their Cardboard Kid or another they see in the community on April 9 and share it via social media using #cardboardkidsSA on Twitter, Instagram and Facebook to build a dialogue about this pressing problem.
 
“Child abuse is an issue that is too big for one agency to tackle, rather we as a community must rally together to learn how to identify it, report it and zealously punish those who target and harm our most vulnerable citizens,” Abernethy said.
 
ChildSafe is Bexar County’s only Children’s Advocacy Center. Its staff performs forensic interviews, crisis intervention, case management services, counseling, and advanced therapy to aid in the healing of child victims of abuse and neglect. And they work with Child Protective Services, law enforcement, and prosecutors to bring perpetrators to justice.
 
To learn more about Cardboard Kids, visit www.cardboardkidsSA.com. For more information about ChildSafe, please visit www.childsafe-sa.org or call (210) 675-9000.

node.title: ChildSafe Kicks-Off National Child Abuse Prevention Month

node.content:
Nissan of Boerne, a brand new Nissan dealership located in Boerne, Texas, hosted its Grand Opening Gala and ribbon cutting on Thursday, March 5. And The PM Group was right there to celebrate this auspicious opening, as Nissan of Boerne’s new adverting agency.
 
Located at 31805 IH 10 West in Boerne, Texas, this is no small car dealership, it’s the largest Nissan dealership in San Antonio (based on square footage). Housed in this 60,000 square foot mega dealership are 33 state-of-the-art service bays, over 600 new and pre-owned vehicles. It will eventually bring up to 100 new jobs to the area.
 
The dealership, which serves San Antonio and the surrounding communities of New Braunfels, San Marcos, Bandera, Kerrville, Fredericksburg and Hondo, offers complimentary amenities including shuttle service; loaner cars; guest WiFi; refreshments; a very comfortable guest lounge and a fun play area for children. 
 
The broadcast from The PM Group has already hit the airwaves and there is more to come as we shift into high gear.

node.content:
Every year, The PM Group and The Wood Agency host their annual Saturday Night FUN-Raiser charitable gala. The event brings together some outstanding San Antonio non-profit organizations to benefit from a night of music, food and “fun-raising.”  A record $175,000 was raised for this year’s benefiting charities – ChildSafe, San Antonio Pets Alive, San Antonio Youth Educational Scholarship Foundation, UTSA Athletic Foundation, San Antonio Sports Foundation, and Wounded Warriors. 
 
“For the 7th consecutive year we have set a new record from this multi-charity event,” stated PM Group CEO Bob Wills. “I cannot express how appreciative we are for making this event such a great success,” Wills added.
 
This year’s casino night event was held at the Hyatt Regency Hill Country Resort and attended by more than 450 individuals from the San Antonio & South Texas corporate, nonprofit, and social communities.
 
Kim Abernethy, CEO of ChildSafe, had this to say about this very unique fundraiser……“ChildSafe was honored to be selected as a beneficiary of the charitable fundraiser presented by the PM Group & Wood Agency. This was a fabulous event and our staff and board are so grateful for the gift we received. ChildSafe will be able to provide more than 100 clients a full spectrum of care and services to help them heal from abuse and neglect.”
 
“Being part of this wonderful event that impacts the community in such a huge way, is a point of great pride for all of us in The Wood Agency,” commented Skip Wood, President of The Wood Agency.
 
We want to thank everyone that helped make the 2015 Fun-Raiser a great success with a special thank you to all of our attendees and sponsors whose generous donations made the evening possible.
node.excerpt: Every year, The PM Group and The Wood Agency host their annual Saturday Night FUN-Raiser charitable gala. The event brings together some outstanding San Antonio non-profit organizations to benefit from a night of music, food and “fun-raising.”  A record $175,000 was raised for this year’s benefiting charities – ChildSafe, San Antonio Pets Alive, San Antonio Youth Educational Scholarship… Continue reading Agency Fundraiser Sets Record with $175,000 for Local Charities!
node.title: Agency Fundraiser Sets Record with $175,000 for Local Charities!

seo.title: Rentrak Redefines TV Audiences | The PM Group
seo.metaDesc: Rentrak, a player in the quest to provide a better measurement for TV viewers, is redefining audiences to provide better insight to advertising agencies.
seo.opengraphTitle: Rentrak Redefines TV Audiences | The PM Group
seo.opengraphDescription: Rentrak, a player in the quest to provide a better measurement for TV viewers, is redefining audiences to provide better insight to advertising agencies.

schema.raw: {"@context":"https://schema.org","@graph":[{"@type":"WebSite","@id":"/#website","url":"/","name":"The PM Group","description":"San Antonio\u2019s Largest Advertising and Marketing Agency","potentialAction":[{"@type":"SearchAction","target":{"@type":"EntryPoint","urlTemplate":"/?s={search_term_string}"},"query-input":"required name=search_term_string"}],"inLanguage":"en-US"},{"@type":"ImageObject","@id":"/rentrak/#primaryimage","inLanguage":"en-US","url":"/static/328c76df2f589ca43b30b28f2b085859/rentrak-logo1.jpg","contentUrl":"/static/328c76df2f589ca43b30b28f2b085859/rentrak-logo1.jpg","width":300,"height":140,"caption":"Rentrak Logo"},{"@type":"WebPage","@id":"/rentrak/#webpage","url":"/rentrak/","name":"Rentrak Redefines TV Audiences | The PM Group","isPartOf":{"@id":"/#website"},"primaryImageOfPage":{"@id":"/rentrak/#primaryimage"},"datePublished":"2014-07-09T22:31:09+00:00","dateModified":"2021-06-24T17:19:20+00:00","author":{"@id":"/#/schema/person/bfa6a54a5572436cd2cb19e8c218e9bd"},"description":"Rentrak, a player in the quest to provide a better measurement for TV viewers, is redefining audiences to provide better insight to advertising agencies.","breadcrumb":{"@id":"/rentrak/#breadcrumb"},"inLanguage":"en-US","potentialAction":[{"@type":"ReadAction","target":["/rentrak/"]}]},{"@type":"BreadcrumbList","@id":"/rentrak/#breadcrumb","itemListElement":[{"@type":"ListItem","position":1,"name":"Rentrak Redefines Audiences"}]},{"@type":"Person","@id":"/#/schema/person/bfa6a54a5572436cd2cb19e8c218e9bd","name":"NTWebDev","image":{"@type":"ImageObject","@id":"/#personlogo","inLanguage":"en-US","url":"https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/15b70540d434acb379a7edf285a9a565?s=96&d=mm&r=g","contentUrl":"https://secure.gravatar.com/avatar/15b70540d434acb379a7edf285a9a565?s=96&d=mm&r=g","caption":"NTWebDev"},"sameAs":["http://thepmgroup.flywheelsites.com"]}]}

node.content:
Rentrak, an emerging player in the quest to provide better measurement for TV viewers, recently said it would use new descriptions for two of its products after consulting with The Media Rating Council – an industry group that monitors and audits ratings-service operations.
 
 
Rentrak says its total television service measures more than 13 million “return-path” TV homes and 29 million televisions, provided by a number of multichannel video programming distributors, allowing for reporting in more than 99% of residential Zip codes. By 2015, Rentrak estimates it will have approximately 26 million TV homes and almost 60 million TVs providing viewing data.
 
 
There is speculation that some media companies believe the presence of a competitor in the market gives them leverage with Nielsen, which is the dominant source of audience data and whose analytics form the basis of how TV is bought and sold by advertisers and TV networks.
 
 
Nielsen, which has long been the industry leader for TV research, still provides the ratings currency upon which national TV advertising is bought and sold. Nielsen collects that data through surveys of a sample of U.S. homes. But broadcasters, which have complained that Nielsen’s numbers understate their true ratings, can use deals Rentrak is putting together to put pressure on Nielsen. Typically when broadcasters hire Rentrak they also keep using Nielsen.
 
 
Also appealing about Rentrak is that it offers the ability to tie viewership to consumer buying habits, which can be a great advantage to marketers planning out local ads. Rentrak has struck agreements with cable and satellite providers to collect data from millions of set-top boxes in different markets. It matches that data with other data sets about consumer purchases.
 
 
With viewership data beyond age and gender in higher demand, Rentrak says it can offer marketers insights on everything from viewer political affiliation to their likelihood of purchasing a vehicle. Nielsen also has competing consumer research products that match their data with credit card, consumer package good, and automotive purchases.
 
 
Experts say that while Nielsen’s survey is unparalleled on a national scale, Rentrak on the local level can provide some marketers and TV companies with additional granularity on consumer habits they cherish. The PM Group utilizes multiple tools to determine the best media solutions for our clients, Rentrak is just one of the tools in our arsenal.

node.title: Rentrak Redefines Audiences

node.content:
The PM Group recently helped long-time friend, NewTek with production of materials for their part in the 2014 NAB Show in Las Vegas.
 
The NAB Show is the world’s largest electronic media show covering filmed entertainment and the development, management and delivery of content across all mediums. NewTek is one of more than 93,000 Attendees from 156 countries and 1,550+ Exhibitors. NAB Show is billed as “the ultimate marketplace for digital media and entertainment.”
 
NewTek Creative Director, Marion DeWall, said this about the work the PM Group did for her company. “When NewTek needed to work with an outside agency, I turned to the creative team I could trust—The PM Group. They are top-performers, fast, accurate, and can be trusted not only to do the job exactly right—but to “plus” the creative, and execute with pixel perfection. They are amazing.”
NewTek is an industry leader at making it easy and cost-effective for anyone to produce quality television content. Whether it’s live production and streaming, slow motion and instant replay, motion graphics and visual effects, NewTek makes a range of tools to help get the job done. Their three leading products are:  TriCaster – All-in-one, integrated live production system that offers the power of a live network studio. 3Play – Slow motion and instant replay system for sports producers at all levels. LightWave 3D – Industry-leading 3D animation and visual effects software used by artists working in television, feature and short film, architecture and design, games and print.
NewTek has plans to use The PM Group for several print production assignments in the near future.

node.content:
We have been fortunate over the years to have earned a good reputation with marketing sports franchises. From award-winning television commercials created for the San Antonio Spurs and Rampage Hockey to campaigns for the WNBA Silver Stars, there’s not much we haven’t tackled. Today we’re proud to announce that The PM Group will be representing another icon in sports, Team Express, with strategic planning, advertising, creative and media buying for their retail business.
Team Express is a distributor of sporting goods and athletic equipment, footwear and apparel. They have a thriving internet and on-line catalog business but The PM Group has been hired to help draw people to their brick and mortar retail store in San Antonio, TX.
The company started in 1990 has been dedicated to providing fanatical baseball, softball and football customers with incredible service and ease of shopping. Their audience is defined as the serious consumer and team athletes who demand the best performance products at reasonable prices. They pride themselves on having the most knowledgeable customer service team and the broadest selection of sports equipment products in the world.
The first assignment for Team Express is a television and radio campaign that will show how those that are serious about their game are serious about coming to Team Express for the right equipment and the right advice. The television will be shot and edited by The PM Group’s sister company Quarter Moon Productions.

seo.opengraphTitle: PEW Research - Women Dominate Social Networks | The PM Group
seo.opengraphDescription: Women and social media have developed a close relationship. PEW research shows they use it much more than men. How does this effect a campaign's strategy?

node.content:
Depending on the product the decision maker during the purchase process can vary between men or women. But when it comes to social media consumption the fairer sex is putting a lot of ground between themselves and their male counterparts.
According to numbers provided by Pew Research and Nielsen women outnumber men in activity on five of the six top social networks. It breaks down something like this – 25 percent more women use Pinterest compared to the guys. Ten percent more women use Facebook, eight percent more women used Tumblr.
So what about Instagram and Twitter?  Here, the numbers narrow a bit – with the gaps being five percent and one percent, respectively. LinkedIn is the one social network site where men outnumber women, but the difference was only five percent.
Also, more women are consuming their news online – 58 percent get their news on Facebook and 52 percent on G+. The guys?  42 and 48 percent of men get their news from the same respective sources.
Women aren’t just breezing through these sites; they use them more. They show support 10 percent more, access 17 percent more offers, and they even comment three percent more than all of those opinionated guys.
Bottom line, reaching women today requires a good social media plan and follow through. And The PM Group has the online expertise to help you make an impact with your audience whether it’s men or women.

node.content:
National Pancake Day has come and gone. But as the advertising agency that does television, print, radio and public relations for IHOP in San Antonio and print and event planning for Pioneer Brands we thought you should know some facts about the incomparable hotcake, griddlecake, or flapjack.
 

  1. 2.5 tons of pancakes are consumed in the US a year. That’s 75 Billion pancakes. That begs the question “Got Milk?”
  2. A silver dollar pancake refers to a pancake about two to three inches (5 to 7 cm) in diameter, or just a bit bigger than the pre-1979 Silver Dollar coins in the United States, for which they are named.
  3. In Britain and the Commonwealth, pancakes are associated with Shrove Tuesday commonly known as Pancake Day. Across the pond this was the day when perishable ingredients had to be used up before the fasting period of Lent began.
  4. Archaeological evidence suggests that pancakes are probably the earliest and most widespread cereal food eaten in prehistoric societies.
  5. In Finland pancakes are usually eaten as dessert with whipped cream and/or pancake-jam, sugar or vanilla ice-cream.
  6. Greek pancakes are called tiganites. They are slightly thicker than crêpes and can be sweet or savory.
  7. In parts of Scotland pancakes are also referred to as drop scones or dropped scones because you drop dollops of mixture onto the cooking surface.
  8. There you have it a few little know facts that you can wow your friends and family with at your next breakfast gathering.

 
Feeling hungry yet? Take a look at some of our mouth watering restaurant work! Now if you want to know how to have your product sell like hotcakes, you should give the largest advertising agency in San Antonio a call.

node.content:
Saturday, February 15th, The San Antonio Advertising Federation held their annual award show at the McNay Art Museum. There were hundreds of traditional and interactive pieces, ads, television and radio commercials entered from most of the agencies and design firms in San Antonio. From all those entries awards were given out as Bronze, Silver, Gold, Best of Category and Best of Show. The entries that won a Silver or Gold will advance to the next level of judging and if they win Gold there they will advance to the National Addy Awards.
 
The PM Group and their sister company, The Wood Agency received a total of 10 awards.
 
The PM Group, took home two Silver and one Bronze award. The Silver awards were for package design created for the Taco Bell/Fiesta Texas Iron Rattler summer promotion. The package was the Taco 12 pack holder. The other Silver was for a “Brand Bible” that was created for our White Wings client. This Brand Bible was created for the Guenther Client to help everyone internally better understand the White Wings brand and what it stood for. A similar “Bible” was done for the Guenther client “Morrison” and it received a Bronze.
 
The Wood Agency took home 7 awards. All of these were for the work on the Methodist 50th Anniversary TV campaign that was shot and produced by Sister company Quarter Moon Productions. The awards were: Gold for the :15 oncology spot titled “Not There Anymore”. There were two silvers for the other :15 “No Louder Than A Heartbeat” and the :30 “Can’t Be Complacent”. The final four were bronze – one for the :60 Anthem spot and one for the overall campaign. The final two bronze were for “Experience” & “What If”.
 
Congratulations to everyone involved.
node.excerpt: Saturday, February 15th, The San Antonio Advertising Federation held their annual award show at the McNay Art Museum. There were hundreds of traditional and interactive pieces, ads, television and radio commercials entered from most of the agencies and design firms in San Antonio. From all those entries awards were given out as Bronze, Silver, Gold,… Continue reading Agency Seizes Gold
